title: RBI MPC Meet June 3–5: Will Repo Rate Hold at 5.25%?
summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee meets June 3–5 with markets widely expecting rates to hold at 5.25%. Crude oil prices and sticky inflation are the key factors. The decision is announced June 5.

coverages.headline: RBI Expected to Hold Rates at 5.25% as Inflation Stays Elevated
coverages.excerpt: The Reserve Bank of India is widely expected to keep its benchmark lending rate unchanged at 5.25% when the monetary policy committee concludes its three-day meeting on June 5, with inflation remaining the primary concern.
coverages.bias_notes: Wire-service neutrality. States the consensus expectation without amplifying it. Uses 'widely expected' — correctly attributing the prediction to market consensus rather than stating it as fact.

coverages.headline: RBI Rate Decision June 5: What It Means for Your Home Loan, FD, and EMI
coverages.excerpt: Markets expect the RBI to hold rates steady at 5.25% amid inflation concerns tied to crude oil prices; announcement due June 5.
coverages.bias_notes: Personalises a macro policy story ('your home loan', 'your EMI') — a common TOI technique to drive clicks from retail readers. Nothing factually wrong, but the frame shifts focus from the policy decision to its consumer impact before it has even happened.

coverages.headline: MPC Meet: RBI Faces Inflation-Growth Dilemma as Crude Prices Weigh on Outlook
coverages.excerpt: The central bank faces competing pressures — sticky core inflation on one side, a slowing consumption growth rate on the other — making this MPC meeting more consequential than the expected hold suggests.
coverages.bias_notes: Contextualises the decision as a genuine dilemma rather than a foregone conclusion. Points to 'sticky core inflation' and 'slowing consumption' — both real data points — giving readers more analytical depth than outlets that simply report the consensus expectation.
